---
title: "Exercise 02 — Python Testing with pytest"
format: html
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
🇫🇷 [Version française](../../../exercises/02_python/README.qmd)
:::

## Objective

Test a Python function that loads and validates a TSV file of tabular data.

## Quick Reference — pytest

Documentation: <https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/>

| Element | Syntax |
|---------|---------|
| Simple assertion | `assert expr` |
| Expected exception | `with pytest.raises(ValueError):` |
| Match on message | `pytest.raises(ValueError, match="texte")` |
| Numeric approximation | `assert val == pytest.approx(3.14)` |

---

## Context

Before processing a data file, we want to ensure that:

- the file exists and is readable
- it contains data (not just a header)
- values are numeric and positive
- identifiers are unique

## Data

```
training/data/records.tsv            # Valid TSV: 20 rows (id, group, value)
training/data/invalid_records.tsv    # Non-numeric value
training/data/empty.tsv              # Header only, no data
training/data/duplicated_records.tsv # Id S01 duplicated
training/data/missing.tsv            # Non-existent file (triggers FileNotFoundError)
```

## Starting Code

`training/pipelines/minimal/scripts/parse_records.py`:

```python
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd


def load_records(path: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    p = Path(path)
    if not p.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}")
    df = pd.read_csv(p, sep="\t")
    if df.empty:
        raise ValueError(f"File has no data rows: {path}")
    return df


def validate_records(df: pd.DataFrame) -> None:
    required = {"id", "group", "value"}
    missing = required - set(df.columns)
    if missing:
        raise ValueError(f"Missing columns: {missing}")
    if not pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(df["value"]):
        raise ValueError("Column 'value' must be numeric")
    if (df["value"] < 0).any():
        raise ValueError("Column 'value' contains negative values")
    duplicated = df[df["id"].duplicated(keep=False)]["id"].unique().tolist()
    if duplicated:
        raise ValueError(f"Duplicate ids: {duplicated}")
```

## Exercise

Complete `tests/pytest/test_parse_records.py`:

```python
import pytest
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from parse_records import load_records, validate_records

DATA = Path("training/data")


def test_load_valid_file():
    # TODO : vérifier shape (20, 3) et colonnes ["id", "group", "value"]
    pass


def test_load_missing_file():
    # TODO : vérifier FileNotFoundError
    pass


def test_load_empty_file():
    # TODO : vérifier ValueError sur empty.tsv
    pass


def test_validate_ok():
    # TODO : vérifier qu'aucune exception n'est levée sur records.tsv
    pass


def test_validate_non_numeric_value():
    # TODO : vérifier ValueError sur invalid_records.tsv
    pass


def test_validate_duplicate_ids():
    # TODO : vérifier ValueError sur duplicated_records.tsv
    pass


def test_all_values_non_negative():
    # TODO : vérifier l'invariant (df["value"] >= 0).all()
    pass


# Bonus — tests paramétrés
# Les quatre cas d'erreur ci-dessous ont la même structure.
# TODO : les regrouper avec @pytest.mark.parametrize
@pytest.mark.parametrize("path,exc", [
    # ("empty.tsv",              ValueError),
    # ("invalid_records.tsv",    ValueError),
    # ("duplicated_records.tsv", ValueError),
    # ("missing.tsv",            FileNotFoundError),
])
def test_inputs_invalides(path, exc):
    with pytest.raises(exc):
        load_records(DATA / path)
```

## Expected Result

```
tests/pytest/test_parse_records.py .......  [100%]
7 passed in 0.3s
```

## Bonus — Coverage

```bash
pytest --cov=parse_records tests/pytest/test_parse_records.py
```

Note that 100% coverage does not guarantee the absence of bugs:
an assertion that is too vague (`assert result is not None`) can cover
a line without proving anything.

## Solution

→ See [solution.qmd](solution.qmd)
